items.note: Spain and Argentina meet at MetLife at 3 p.m. ET under an FBI-led operation its director called the biggest in the bureau’s history, with the result still hours away.
Iran is led by Mojtaba Khamenei, appointed in March after a U.S.–Israeli strike killed his father, Ali Khamenei, on 28 February, the killing that opened this war.
The Strait of Hormuz runs far below normal as U.S. strikes press Iranian maritime capabilities. Traders warn the buffers that absorbed the first disruption are depleted.
Iran struck U.S. assets in Kuwait overnight. Kuwait said the country came under sustained attack with intercepts and injuries to responders and oil-sector workers.
Two U.S. service members were killed and one went missing on 17 July defending a base against Iranian missiles and drones, the strike that set off the latest U.S. wave.

items.what: The ferry MV Barima, carrying 116 people from Georgetown toward Port Kaituma, capsized near Iron Punt on the night of 18 July. By the afternoon of 19 July between 53 and 67 passengers had been rescued while a state-led search continued for the rest.

A magnitude-5.5 earthquake struck near Chupaca in the Junín region on the evening of 18 July at shallow depth. Peruvian officials reported at least five people killed and 21 injured, with an aftershock following soon after.

Venezuelan authorities put the death toll from the 24 June twin earthquakes off La Guaira at 5,069. About 16,740 people were injured and some 20,000 remained displaced after more than 1,300 recorded aftershocks.

U.S. Marshals arrested Andrew and Tristan Tate in Miami on 18 July on a U.K. extradition request. The Crown Prosecution Service announced additional sexual-offence charges against the brothers spanning alleged conduct from 2010 to 2017.

West African heads of state convened the ECOWAS 69th summit in Freetown on 19 July. A rotating-chair handover was expected as the group weighed its fractured relationship with the Sahel states that quit last year.

Canadian wildfire haze that blanketed the U.S. Northeast into 18 July was forecast to thin as thunderstorms moved through. Air quality was expected to improve toward moderate before the final’s afternoon kickoff.
